## Vault 2.0.0 

### Summary

Vault Enterprise 2.0 focuses on reducing manual setup and operational friction
for teams that manage identity, secrets, and encryption across large
environments. The release helps operators connect Vault to existing identity
systems, deliver workload identity in SPIFFE-based environments, and sync
secrets to external platforms without depending on long-lived cloud
credentials.

This release also helps teams manage distributed infrastructure with less
custom glue code and fewer one-off workflows. Operators can standardize
credential rotation, support multi-region key management, automate Linux local
account management, and use more guided UI workflows when they onboard teams,
create policies, and discover Vault capabilities.

### New features

- **[SCIM identity provisioning](/vault/docs/enterprise/scim-overview)** - Automate
  identity lifecycle management by provisioning entities and groups in Vault from
  external identity platforms.
- [SPIFFE JWT-SVID support](/vault/docs/secrets/spiffe) lets authenticated
  workloads request JWT-SVIDs from Vault so that they can participate in
<<<<<<< HEAD
- **[SPIFFE JWT-SVID support](/vault/docs/secrets/spiffe)** - Let authenticated
  workloads request JWT-SVIDs from Vault so they can participate in SPIFFE-based
  identity workflows.
- **[Visual policy generator](/vault/docs/ui/visual-policy-editor)** - Create
  policies faster and reduce manual policy authoring by generating ACL policy
  snippets from the Vault GUI.
- **[Feature introduction pages](/vault/docs/about-vault/why-use-vault)** - The
  Vault GUI now provides guided overviews for key Vault capabilities that help
  you understand core features without leaving the GUI.
- **[Namespace onboarding workflow](/vault/docs/enterprise/namespaces)** - Answer
  a few key questions in the Vault GUI to create new namespaces then continue in
  the GUI, CLI, or Terraform.
- **[Secret Sync](/vault/docs/sync)** - Use workload identity federation (WIF)
  to sync secrets to external systems without storing static credentials.
- **[AWS KMS multi-region keys](/vault/docs/secrets/key-management/awskms#multi-region-keys)** -
  Create and replicate managed keys across AWS regions so you can support
  multi-region encryption and disaster recovery workflows.
- **Local accounts secrets engine** - Use Vault to manage Linux local accounts
  and rotate credentials for automated local account credential management.
- **[LDAP static role rotation enhancements](/vault/docs/secrets/ldap)** - 
  Manage LDAP static credentials with more flexibility by adding initial
  passwords, self-managed rotation, schedules, and retry controls.
- **[Rotation policies](/vault/docs/concepts/rotation-policies)** - Standardize
  how Vault handles failed automated rotations by defining reusable retry
  behavior for supported roles.
=======
  SPIFFE-based identity workflows.
- [Visual policy generator](/vault/docs/ui/visual-policy-editor) lets you
  generate ACL policy snippets from the Vault GUI so that you can create
  policies faster and reduce manual policy authoring.
- [Feature introduction pages](/vault/docs/about-vault/why-use-vault) add
  guided overviews for key Vault capabilities so that users can understand
  core features without leaving the UI.
- [Namespace onboarding](/vault/docs/enterprise/namespaces) adds a guided
  workflow for namespace setup so that operators can answer a few questions
  and continue in the UI, CLI, or Terraform.
- [Secret Sync](/vault/docs/sync) supports workload identity federation
  (WIF) so that you can sync secrets to external systems without storing
  static credentials.
- [AWS KMS multi-region keys](/vault/docs/secrets/key-management/awskms#multi-region-keys)
  let you create and replicate managed keys across AWS regions so that you
  can support multi-region encryption and disaster recovery workflows.
- [Vault Agent support for public ACME certificate workflows](/vault/docs/agent-and-proxy/agent/pki-external-ca)
  lets applications use public TLS certificates without implementing ACME
  logic. Vault Agent requests and renews certificates through Vault, handles
  HTTP-01 challenge responses, and writes certificates and private keys to
  disk for application use.
- [Envelope encryption for large files and streams](/vault/docs/secrets/transit/envelope-encryption)
  lets applications encrypt data in place without sending large artifacts or
  streaming plaintext through Transit. Vault provides and protects the data
  keys used for encryption so authorized clients can decrypt the data later.
- Local accounts secrets engine lets Vault manage Linux local accounts and
  rotate credentials so that you can automate local account credential
  management.
- [LDAP static role rotation enhancements](/vault/docs/secrets/ldap) add
  initial passwords, self-managed rotation, schedules, and retry controls so
  that you can manage LDAP static credentials with more flexibility.
- [Rotation policies](/vault/docs/concepts/rotation-policies) let you define
  reusable retry behavior for supported roles so that you can standardize how
  Vault handles failed automated rotations.

### Bug fixes and security patches

- Credential rotation: [Vault Agent template backoff](/vault/docs/agent-and-proxy/agent/template)
  reduces aggressive retries during secret rotation so that agents create less
  unnecessary network traffic and server load.
- identity: Repair the integrity of duplicate and/or dangling entity aliases.

### Improvements

- The Vault UI navigation now organizes features around common operator tasks so
  users can find related capabilities faster.
- In-product feature descriptions explain what features do and when to use
  them so that users can evaluate capabilities in context.
<<<<<<< HEAD
- Secrets inventory reporting can scan existing AWS and Azure environments to
  seed reporting data so teams can get value from reporting without
  starting from an empty inventory.

>>>>>>> 5d2922bac (docs: update Vault 2.0 release notes)
- [Azure secrets engine](/vault/docs/secrets/azure) adds role metadata
  support, separates static credential import, and lowers the minimum TTL for
  static roles to 30 days so operators can manage Azure roles with more
  consistency.
- [Event notifications](/vault/docs/concepts/events) support subscriptions
  from secondary clusters for events on the primary cluster so
  multi-cluster deployments can react with lower latency.
- [External plugin management](/vault/docs/plugins/register) is easier in the
  Vault GUI so operators can manage external versions of built-in plugins
  without relying only on the API or CLI.
- [IBM Passport Advantage Online license support](/vault/docs/configuration/license-entitlement)
  lets Vault Enterprise use IBM Passport Advantage Online license keys so
  customers can reduce licensing fulfillment delays.
- Support for an additional licensing framework aligns pricing with workload,
  automation, and machine identity growth so billing better reflects
  platform usage.
- [JWT auth](/vault/docs/auth/jwt) updates improve JWT-based authentication workflows.
- [Rotation retries](/vault/docs/concepts/rotation-policies) use exponential
